---
title: "Assessing Professor Effectiveness"
subtitle: "What 89,893 RateMyProfessor records say about gender bias, teaching tags, and what drives a rating"
---

::: {.deck}
A reproducible statistical analysis of the RateMyProfessor dataset: is there a pro‑male
rating bias, which teaching "tags" are gendered, and how well can we predict a professor's
rating — or whether they get a "pepper"? Every figure below is generated by the
[`ape`](https://github.com/deepanshumody/Analysis_RMP_Ratings) package; click **Code** to see how.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="stat-row">
  <div class="stat"><div class="num">89,893</div><div class="lab">Professors analyzed</div></div>
  <div class="stat"><div class="num">11</div><div class="lab">Questions answered</div></div>
  <div class="stat"><div class="num">42</div><div class="lab">Passing tests</div></div>
  <div class="stat"><div class="num">0.81</div><div class="lab">Classifier AUC</div></div>
</div>
```

## Key findings {#findings}

```{python}
#| label: tbl-findings
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import display
from ape import questions


def show(figs, *keys):
    """Display only the named figures; close the rest so nothing leaks."""
    for k, f in figs.items():
        if k not in keys:
            plt.close(f)
    for k in keys:
        display(figs[k])
    plt.close("all")


q1, _ = questions.q1(); q2, _ = questions.q2(); q3, _ = questions.q3()
q4, _ = questions.q4(); q5, _ = questions.q5()
q7, _ = questions.q7(); q8, _ = questions.q8(); q9, _ = questions.q9()
q10, _ = questions.q10(); q11, _ = questions.q11()

rows = [
    ("Q1  Pro-male rating bias?", "Yes, small", f"MWU p={q1['mwu_p_one_sided_greater']:.1e}",
     f"d={q3['cohen_d']:.3f}"),
    ("Q2  Difference in spread?", "Yes", f"Levene p={q2['levene_p']:.4f}",
     f"var ratio={q3['variance_ratio']:.2f}"),
    ("Q4  Gendered tags?", f"{q4['n_significant_fdr']}/20 tags", "FDR @ α=0.005",
     "top: " + ", ".join(q4['most_gendered'][:2])),
    ("Q5  Difference in difficulty?", "No", f"MWU p={q5['mwu_p']:.2f}", "—"),
    ("Q7  Rating ~ numeric", "Strong", f"R²={q7['best_r2']:.2f}",
     f"top: {q7['top_predictor']}"),
    ("Q8  Rating ~ tags", "Good", f"R²={q8['best_r2']:.2f}",
     f"top: {q8['top_predictor']}"),
    ("Q9  Difficulty ~ tags", "Moderate", f"R²={q9['best_r2']:.2f}",
     f"top: {q9['top_predictor']}"),
    ("Q10 Predict 'pepper'", "Good", f"AUC={q10['auc']:.2f}", "balanced + Youden J"),
    ("Q11 NY vs NJ ratings", "No difference", f"MWU p={q11['mwu_p']:.2f}", "—"),
]
plt.close("all")  # the q-calls above each built a figure; drop them
pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=["Question", "Verdict", "Statistic", "Detail"])
```

## At a glance

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
```{python}
#| label: fig-rating
#| fig-cap: "Average rating by gender (professors with ≥10 ratings). Men sit slightly higher — a small but statistically significant gap."
_, figs = questions.q1()
show(figs, "q1_rating_by_gender")
```
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
```{python}
#| label: fig-roc
#| fig-cap: "ROC for the 'pepper' classifier (AUC ≈ 0.81). Standardization is fit inside the pipeline; the threshold is chosen by Youden's J."
_, figs = questions.q10()
show(figs, "q10_roc")
```
:::

:::

## How to read this site

- **[Methods](methods.qmd)** — the data, cleaning choices, the α = 0.005 threshold, and our statistical approach (FDR control across many tests, honest out-of-sample model evaluation, directional tests where the hypothesis calls for one).
- **[Gender bias](gender.qmd)** — Q1–Q3, Q5–Q6: bias in the mean, the spread, and effect sizes, plus difficulty.
- **[Tags](tags.qmd)** — Q4, Q8, Q9: which tags are gendered, and tag-driven rating/difficulty models.
- **[Models](models.qmd)** — Q7 and Q10: predicting rating and "pepper", with from-scratch regression validated against scikit-learn.
- **[Extensions](extensions.qmd)** — the NY-vs-NJ bonus and how we'd push the analysis further.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## About this project
NYU **DS-GA 1001** capstone, group **CAP 85** (Deepanshu Mody, Evan Beck, Samarth Agarwal).
The analysis is packaged as tested, importable code (`ape`) with a reproducible pipeline.
Regression and classification (Q7–Q10) and this engineering write-up are Deepanshu's contributions.
:::
